pattern: A one-dimensional dynamic program where each prefix decides how far back the current shelf extends, minimizing total stacked height under a width cap.
hints: Books must stay in order, so the only choice is where each new shelf begins.
Define the best total height to place the first i books; the i-th book ends some shelf whose start you must choose.
For dp[i], try extending the last shelf leftward over books j..i while their thickness fits shelfWidth, tracking the max height of that group, and take the minimum of dp[j-1] plus that height.

edge_inputs.description: first example with a non-trivial grouping
each book exactly fills the width so all go on separate shelves
all books fit on one shelf, height is the max
a tall book is better isolated

stress.description: moderate sequence of random books with a tight width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    shelfWidth = rng.randrange(5, 30)
    books = [[rng.randrange(1, shelfWidth + 1), rng.randrange(1, 50)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [books, shelfWidth]
stress.note: thickness never exceeds shelfWidth, respecting the constraint
